defect_areas: inbound return-call routing (voice-registration eligibility + last-dialer preference + initiator carry)
voice-presence persistence (voice_registered on wire + presence record, preserved across flag-less updates)
user-facing error copy (AI non-US, direct-call not configured, voice-token outage)
API access-log noise suppression (QuietPollingAccessLogFilter over 3 polling paths, 2xx/3xx only)
excluded_from_scope: loangen-app/** (frontend TSX/TS changes in the same commit)
gold_test_files: loangen-agent/tests/test_logging_setup.py (new; commit's own test with base-missing import moved into bodies)
env_rationale: Reuses the round-1 10-var Twilio/CALLS_* dummy set. Opens the inbound/direct gates and satisfies pydantic Settings validation when the calls + smbcontacts services import. The routing tests additionally patch agent.services.calls.service.inbound_calls_enabled directly, so they are hermetic regardless of the gate. All credentials are dummy stubs; the suite never constructs a Twilio or Cartesia client.

leak_checks.visible_base_suite_green: 20 passed (test_inbound_calls.py + test_required_docs.py at base, no test_patch)

fairness_audit: Every f2p asserts observable behavior, not the oracle's implementation. Collaborators are patched at their defining module (agent.services.calls.inbound._find_last_outbound_to_phone, presence.get_redis_client, smbcontacts.service._get_lender_contact/_normalize_contact_phone/direct_call_configured, service.direct_calls_enabled) or replaced with typed fakes (a real _FakeRedis with get/set/scan_iter; a MagicMock last-outbound call session with explicit string attributes). No `patch(consumer.Collaborator, MagicMock())` module-attr patching. The alternative-implementation audit was run in-image: a materially different oracle (InboundRoute field renamed `initiator_session` with a default, pick kwarg renamed `preferred` with a comprehension filter, preserve logic via `get_presence(...) or {}`, reworded user messages, and a string-parsing rather than regex log filter) scores reward 1 / 32-32. The only names the hidden suite pins are the two published contract names (`voice_registered` presence/heartbeat key and `agent.core.logging_setup.QuietPollingAccessLogFilter`), both underivable from the base codebase and both allowed under the rule-3 gold-test exception. The routing preference and initiator carry are validated only through emitted TwiML, so the internal field/kwarg names are free.
litmus_result: PASS (with caveat). The instruction is a symptom report: no file paths, no function names, no defect count/shape, no fix mechanism, no threshold/routing tables. The only published identifiers are the wire field `voice_registered` and the test-imported filter class. A strong dev could locate the affected subsystem (the calls services) within a couple of minutes — unavoidable for a faithful report of real user-visible symptoms — but cannot reconstruct the fix without cross-file diagnosis: they must infer that a voice-registration signal has to be threaded wire -> heartbeat -> presence with preserve-on-omit, that routing must both filter on it and prefer the last dialer while carrying the initiator through the resolved route, and must find all three operator-facing messages themselves. This is a categorical improvement over round-1, whose instruction published the exact function signatures, eligibility rule, presence semantics, and dataclass field (the diff in prose).

verification.null: required passed: 18/32, reward 0, every f2p emits a per-test FAILED line (no collection ERRORs)
verification.visible_base_suite: 20 passed (agent-visible tests at base, test_patch not applied)

notes: Hardened rebuild of inbound-call-routing (round-1: sonnet 5/5, TOO EASY because the instruction published a function-level contract). v2 widens to the commit's full backend defect surface (routing + presence + messaging + logging across 9 source files), tests at the observable-behavior level so the internal contract is no longer leaked, and rewrites the instruction as a symptoms-only support escalation. Git history is neutralized to a single import commit so the upstream fix is unrecoverable.
